title: Your first class
instruction:
Classes default to `private` — the opposite of `struct`.

`public:` section is accessible from outside. `private:` is hidden.

Run this — notice how the class controls access.
successMessage: The constructor printed 'created!' when each Dog was made. : name(n), breed(b) is a member initialiser list — the most efficient way to initialise members in C++.

title: Destructor — automatic cleanup
successMessage: Destroyed in reverse order (LIFO — last in, first out). This is why RAII is powerful: database connections, file handles, mutexes — all released automatically, even if an exception occurs.

title: Member initializer list
successMessage: const members MUST be set in the initializer list — they can't be assigned after construction. The member initializer list also avoids default-constructing then assigning — more efficient for non-trivial types.

title: Operator overloading
successMessage: Operator overloading makes your types feel native. friend ostream& operator<< enables cout << a. Vector2D is how game engines represent positions, velocities, and directions.

title: this pointer
successMessage: Method chaining via return *this — each method returns the same object. This is the fluent interface pattern used by string builders, query builders, and configuration APIs everywhere.

title: Build a Matrix class
instruction:
No starter code.

Build a `Matrix2x2` class:
- Fields: `double a, b, c, d` (row 0: a,b; row 1: c,d)
- Constructor with all four values
- `operator+` for matrix addition
- `operator*` for scalar multiplication
- `determinant()` returns `a*d - b*c`
- `friend ostream& operator<<` for printing as `[[a,b],[c,d]]`

Test with two matrices.
hint: Matrix2x2 operator+(const Matrix2x2& o) const { return {a+o.a, b+o.b, c+o.c, d+o.d}; }
successMessage: A 2×2 matrix class with operator overloading. Linear algebra is the foundation of 3D graphics, machine learning, and physics simulations — all built on classes like this. Chapter 12 done!
